other_observers: None; Birds found by Nathan Pieplow and David Waltman
specific_location: Fentress Lake at Neva & Fairways Dr., Lake Valley Subdivision
description: Large white, long-necked waterfowl. Bills of the adults mostly black, long, with slightly concave culmen, with thin reddish-orange line visible along the basal half of the tomia. The juvenile bill had a similar shape, but had more reddish-pink coloration throughout the maxilla. On the adults, the junction of the bill & forehead produced a rather flat curve, while the juvenile showed a mope pointed junction here. The adult birds showed yellow loral patches. One had a tiny, tear-drop shaped yellow spot, while the other had extensive yellow lores (closely matching the "maximum yellow" illustrated by Sibley.) The bird with the extensive yellow lores was a bit smaller than the other adult. The juvenile had a pale grayish patch in its lores, but no yellow. Other than the black eye, the rest of the adults were completely white. The juvenile had a grayish cast over its body, with the most gray in the head.Behaviors: Mostly the birds were feeding by tipping down. Vegetation plucked from the lake's substrate was sometimes visible hanging out of their bills as they tipped back up. A few other waterfowl came and went, such as American Wigeons and Mallards.Call: Not heardPlumage: 2 adults and 1 juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
